title: rubus hirtus subsp. kaltenbachii
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. kaltenbachii, also known as the Kaltenbach's raspberry or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus hirtus plant, a type of raspberry native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its high content of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. kaltenbachii include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and provide relief from symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
